title: podman farm build (on remote builders) complains about "unknown transport" when pushing
body:
### Issue Description

I am attempting to build a multi-platform image by using two podman remote builders (one linux/amd64, and the other linux/arm64/v8).

The command used is:
```bash
podman --log-level=trace farm build --local=false --farm ci-farm --platforms linux/amd64,linux/arm64/v8 --jobs 8 --disable-compression=false --logfile build.log --secret id=mysql,src=/var/folders/rm/k9jt2klx67s1p_c1g28_19gm0000gn/T/tmp.xPf9YWpi0T --tag docker.io/orgname/image_name:v1.1.1 --file Dockerfile .
```

The images on both the builders get build to completion. From the logs (since I am not using `--no-cache`):

```
[linux/amd64@t-reza-x86-builder-2404] STEP 6/6: RUN rm -rf setup_env.sh package_install.sh /tmp/*
[linux/amd64@t-reza-x86-builder-2404] --> Using cache 81bd648e81ce5d3d221bf964a06061507dbef23ebbca4df22297112f54f6e51f
[linux/amd64@t-reza-x86-builder-2404] --> 81bd648e81ce
[linux/amd64@t-reza-x86-builder-2404] 81bd648e81ce5d3d221bf964a06061507dbef23ebbca4df22297112f54f6e51f
...
[linux/arm64/v8@linuxbox24] STEP 6/6: RUN rm -rf setup_env.sh package_install.sh /tmp/*
[linux/arm64/v8@linuxbox24] --> Using cache 2c3b67e83d004aadde5d7f8113d98a6292c7029dddcb2596f6718ced58abd603
[linux/arm64/v8@linuxbox24] --> 2c3b67e83d00
[linux/arm64/v8@linuxbox24] 2c3b67e83d004aadde5d7f8113d98a6292c7029dddcb2596f6718ced58abd603
...
INFO[0010] pushing image 81bd648e81ce5d3d221bf964a06061507dbef23ebbca4df22297112f54f6e51f
INFO[0010] pushing image 2c3b67e83d004aadde5d7f8113d98a6292c7029dddcb2596f6718ced58abd603
...
INFO[0010] pushed image 2c3b67e83d004aadde5d7f8113d98a6292c7029dddcb2596f6718ced58abd603
INFO[0010] pushed image 81bd648e81ce5d3d221bf964a06061507dbef23ebbca4df22297112f54f6e51f
```

The error message:
```
Error: build: building: 2 errors occurred:
	* pushing image {"2c3b67e83d004aadde5d7f8113d98a6292c7029dddcb2596f6718ced58abd603" "docker-archive"} to registry: Invalid image name "docker.io/orgname/image_name:v1.1.1@@unknown-digest@@", unknown transport "docker.io/orgname/image_name"
	* pushing image {"81bd648e81ce5d3d221bf964a06061507dbef23ebbca4df22297112f54f6e51f" "docker-archive"} to registry: Invalid image name "docker.io/orgname/image_name:v1.1.1@@unknown-digest@@", unknown transport "docker.io/orgname/image_name"
```


Somehow, it is trying to parse the transport name from the tag provided in the command.

If I do provide the transport name in the tag like this: `--tag docker://docker.io/orgname/image_name:v1.1.1`, then it complains:
```
creating manifest list "docker://docker.io/orgname/image_name:v1.1.1": creating manifest: invalid image name docker://docker.io/orgname/image_name:v1.1.1: invalid reference format
```

What should be the expected behaviour, or the correct command?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Add two connections to podman for the two remote builders of different architectures. (I am using SSH based connections.)
2. Create a farm, and add both the connections to it.
3. Log into the registry using `podman login`. (Using docker.io, in my case.)
4. Start the podman farm build, with the command as above.

 
The two remote builders are two VMs.
One is using amd64 arch, running on a remote hypervisor.
The other VM is running on my local (Apple M1) using VMWare, on the amd64/v8 arch.

### Describe the results you received

The images on both the builders get build to completion. From the logs (since I am not using `--no-cache`):

```
[linux/amd64@t-reza-x86-builder-2404] STEP 6/6: RUN rm -rf setup_env.sh package_install.sh /tmp/*
[linux/amd64@t-reza-x86-builder-2404] --> Using cache 81bd648e81ce5d3d221bf964a06061507dbef23ebbca4df22297112f54f6e51f
[linux/amd64@t-reza-x86-builder-2404] --> 81bd648e81ce
[linux/amd64@t-reza-x86-builder-2404] 81bd648e81ce5d3d221bf964a06061507dbef23ebbca4df22297112f54f6e51f
...
[linux/arm64/v8@linuxbox24] STEP 6/6: RUN rm -rf setup_env.sh package_install.sh /tmp/*
[linux/arm64/v8@linuxbox24] --> Using cache 2c3b67e83d004aadde5d7f8113d98a6292c7029dddcb2596f6718ced58abd603
[linux/arm64/v8@linuxbox24] --> 2c3b67e83d00
[linux/arm64/v8@linuxbox24] 2c3b67e83d004aadde5d7f8113d98a6292c7029dddcb2596f6718ced58abd603
...
INFO[0010] pushing image 81bd648e81ce5d3d221bf964a06061507dbef23ebbca4df22297112f54f6e51f
INFO[0010] pushing image 2c3b67e83d004aadde5d7f8113d98a6292c7029dddcb2596f6718ced58abd603
...
INFO[0010] pushed image 2c3b67e83d004aadde5d7f8113d98a6292c7029dddcb2596f6718ced58abd603
INFO[0010] pushed image 81bd648e81ce5d3d221bf964a06061507dbef23ebbca4df22297112f54f6e51f
```

The error message:
```
Error: build: building: 2 errors occurred:
	* pushing image {"2c3b67e83d004aadde5d7f8113d98a6292c7029dddcb2596f6718ced58abd603" "docker-archive"} to registry: Invalid image name "docker.io/orgname/image_name:v1.1.1@@unknown-digest@@", unknown transport "docker.io/orgname/image_name"
	* pushing image {"81bd648e81ce5d3d221bf964a06061507dbef23ebbca4df22297112f54f6e51f" "docker-archive"} to registry: Invalid image name "docker.io/orgname/image_name:v1.1.1@@unknown-digest@@", unknown transport "docker.io/orgname/image_name"
```


Somehow, it is trying to parse the transport name from the tag provided in the command.

If I do provide the transport name in the tag like this: `--tag docker://docker.io/orgname/image_name:v1.1.1`, then it complains:
```
creating manifest list "docker://docker.io/orgname/image_name:v1.1.1": creating manifest: invalid image name docker://docker.io/orgname/image_name:v1.1.1: invalid reference format
```

### Describe the results you expected

Expected the manifest of the multi-platform build to be created successfully, and for the multi-platform image to get pushed to the remote registry.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Command invoked from: Apple M1 (macOS Tahoe 26.0 (25A354))

amd64 Builder: OpenStack Nova (Ubuntu 24.04)
arm64 Builder: VMware20,1 (Ubuntu 24.04)

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
A slightly related/relevant issue is https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21338

Though the scenario is different (local insecure registry), the error message is similar.

```
 * pushing image {"063b93b4f7a0c41748e4a457d56b73afc8226682d7fe57a2ee0fbb3fc7e1fea6" "docker-archive"} to registry: Invalid image name "localhost:5000/testbuild@@unknown-digest@@", unknown transport "localhost"
```

Workaround to my problem:

The build works without specifying any tag (building with `latest` tag).

After the image with `latest` tag is built and pushed, used `skopeo copy` to tag and push the image as required.
